earnings_source: EDGAR 10-K/10-Q filing dates (stage5_output/_filing_calendar_cache.parquet) via cik_ticker_map; filing date = earnings-event proxy (assumed scheduled/known in advance for exit logic)
verdict: Dodging earnings HURTS c78q. The hold-through baseline earns 29.9% CAGR / Sharpe 1.14. The best earnings-overlay variant (exit_[-1,+1]_redistribute) gives 29.2% CAGR / Sharpe 1.17, a -0.7pp CAGR change. Attribution: earnings-window [-1,+1] held-name-days are 8.6% of arithmetic return while being 6.1% of held name-days; avg earnings-window day return 0.173% vs normal 0.119% (1.45x). Because earnings days carry disproportionately positive return, flattening into earnings forfeits alpha -> hold through.
caveats: Filing date proxies the earnings call; actual call typically 0-3 trading days earlier. Windows anchored on filing date so true earnings-day effect may be slightly shifted.
Earnings dates assumed known in advance for overlay exits (realistic: earnings are pre-scheduled).
Pre-2010 survivorship caveat inherited from the underlying panel (study window 2011-2026).
